recent_calls.summary: Pat called to schedule a routine maintenance checkup for their air conditioning system. The agent provided available time slots, and Pat booked an appointment for April 15th at 2 PM. The call ended positively with Pat giving a high satisfaction rating and positive feedback about the agent's assistance.

The user, Tiffany Lowery, called to verify her appointment schedule but the agent could not find her appointment in the system. Tiffany chose to leave a message requesting to schedule an appointment for next week, either Tuesday or Wednesday. The agent assured her that the message would be forwarded to the team for follow-up.

The agent, Sophie, contacted Deborah who mentioned she had received a message but had not checked it yet. Deborah decided to check the message and call back later, and the agent offered to connect her to a live agent immediately before transferring the call successfully.
The user, Sunita from Assured Home Warranty, called and requested to speak to the office manager. The agent attempted to connect her to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.
The user requested to speak with a person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Jett called to request that a report from a technician be filed with their home warranty regarding their heating and cooling system, which needs to be replaced. The agent confirmed the details and assured Jett that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly to assist. Jett declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user requested to be connected to CJ, and the agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.
The user Maddie returned a call from Nicole and was connected to a live agent for further assistance. The call was transferred successfully to another agent for handling.

The user provided their service address ZIP code and mentioned the company's location in Wentzville, noting the distance might cause delays. The agent then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Liza from American Home Shield, called to request the immediate cancellation of a work order for air conditioning service. The agent confirmed the details and assured the user that the cancellation request would be passed to the team for processing. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the interaction.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance. No additional information was provided by the user.

The user, Jaclyn Jones, called to sign up for a whole home plan and schedule routine HVAC cleaning and maintenance, including an estimate for a new thermostat and outdoor unit. An appointment was successfully booked for April 15th at 10 AM. Additionally, the user requested a plumbing appointment for drain cleaning, which was noted and will be confirmed by a callback from the team.

Lori Noker called to review an estimate for her furnace HVAC unit and requested a service appointment anytime today. The agent provided available slots for April 14th and 15th, but Lori asked to speak to someone else, leading to a call transfer. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user, Jim Stigemeyer, called to schedule a service appointment for a heat pump showing an error code at his apartment building. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for April 14, 2026, at 2 PM. The user confirmed the booking and the call ended successfully.

The user, Daniel from Nexar, called to introduce his company's dashcam and GPS tracking services and inquired if the agent's company currently uses dashcams. The agent requested a service ZIP code to assist further, which the user provided, but the call ended abruptly after the user repeated questions without receiving a response.

The user called to address heating and cooling issues in two rooms of a recent addition to their home. The agent gathered necessary details, confirmed the user's information, and successfully booked a service appointment for April 13th between 2 PM and 4 PM. The user declined to provide feedback and ended the call.

Megan Rowe inquired about the average cost of a split system unit installation. The agent provided pricing information and offered to have a team member follow up, but Megan decided to have someone else handle the installation and did not provide the service address. The call ended with Megan confirming the company does installations and expressing thanks.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Jose called to inquire about the delay of a scheduled appointment that was supposed to occur between 9 and 11 AM but had not started by after 12 PM. The agent confirmed the contact number and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user called to reconnect with a live agent after a previous call was cut off. The agent acknowledged the issue and successfully transferred the user to the appropriate live agent.

The user called to inquire about a technician appointment scheduled between 10 and 12, noting the technician had not yet arrived. The agent apologized for the delay and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to speak to a specific person but did not engage further after the agent introduced herself and asked how she could help. The call ended abruptly with the user hanging up shortly after.

The call involved Wei, an escalation specialist, clarifying details about a work order for HVAC service, including the homeowner's concerns about paying an out-of-pocket cost before repair. The agent confirmed the correct homeowner name, address, and phone number, and scheduled the service for next Wednesday. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided feedback requesting connection to a live representative instead of AI.
The user returned a call from Budget and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The user called to follow up on a previous service related to a nozzle replacement. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called and requested to speak to someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to request the qualified manufacturer identification number (QMID) for a unit installed in December. The agent confirmed the request and contact details, and assured the user that a human agent would follow up with the information. The call ended after the user acknowledged the confirmation.

The user called to adjust an appointment date related to their home warranty service for air conditioning. They mentioned flexibility with the appointment timing and confirmed the service type as cooling. The call ended shortly after without further details or scheduling confirmation.

Jose called to check on the status of a technician who was supposed to arrive by 11 but was late. The agent confirmed the contact number and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to connect with a technician for their HVAC system service appointment. The agent attempted to assist but the user requested to speak with a human, leading to the call being transferred to a live agent. The transfer was successful.
The user called to ask a question about a quote. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for a residential property, indicating it was not an emergency. The agent attempted to clarify the type of service needed, but the user response was unclear. The call ended without a confirmed service type or appointment.

Walter called to schedule a boiler service appointment due to lack of heat. The agent confirmed details and booked an appointment for April 13th at noon. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings and a request for the service address ZIP code. The user hung up shortly after the agent's introduction.

The user called to schedule a spring AC service check and successfully booked an appointment for April 21st at 8:00 a.m. The user expressed dissatisfaction with the AI assistant's speaking speed and preference for human interaction, leading to a transfer to a live agent. The call ended with the transfer to a human representative.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings. The user hung up shortly after the agent introduced herself.
The user, Elena, called and requested to speak with someone in the office. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after it started, with the user not providing any clear response.

The user called to speak with Nicole about a service call. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to another agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The agent attempted to verify the user's service address ZIP code to assist with the inquiry, but the user was unclear and did not provide the information. The call ended without resolution as the user hung up.

Walter called to schedule a service appointment for his boiler under a home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and assured Walter that a representative would contact him before the service visit, noting his preference for a callback today. The call ended with the agent offering information about maintenance programs.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate agent.

Mike Walkenhorst called back regarding a job interview after receiving a voicemail. The agent clarified that she handles service requests and will forward Mike's information to the hiring team for follow-up. Mike confirmed no further assistance was needed and ended the call.

The user called to schedule a routine maintenance tune-up for both the furnace and outside unit at their home. They provided the service address and preferred appointment window, confirmed contact details, and gave feedback on the call experience. The agent successfully gathered all necessary information and confirmed the appointment details.
The user called back after accidentally hanging up on a previous call. The agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent and successfully transferred the call.

Dr. Maclin, the building principal, provided an update regarding a recent social media threat at Hazelwood Central High School, confirming it was not credible and that appropriate actions are being taken. The message emphasized the importance of safety and cooperation with law enforcement. The call ended with instructions on how to opt out of future messages.

The user, Sally, called to inquire about the appointment time for a technician visit scheduled for the afternoon. The agent confirmed the appointment details and attempted to assist further but then transferred the call to a live agent for additional help.
The user requested to speak immediately with CG or Amy at the office. The agent attempted to transfer the call and successfully connected the user to the requested party.
